campaign.title: Is Your Business Set Up for the Next 5 Years
campaign.subject: The businesses that survive the next 5 years will have this in common
campaign.description: What separates businesses that thrive from ones that struggle

landing_page.ai_goal: Show the business owner what they need to have in place

slides.title: The Next 5 Years

content.headline: The Next 5 Years Will Change Everything
content.subhead: Some businesses will thrive. Others will disappear.

slides.narration: The next five years will change everything about how people find and choose local businesses. Some businesses will thrive—more visible, more efficient, more connected than ever. Others will disappear—unable to adapt, invisible to new discovery methods. The difference isn't luck. It's a few key decisions made now.

slides.title: What's Coming

shifts.impact: How people find businesses changes fundamentally
Customers expect you to know them
Businesses without your account team can't compete on service

slides.narration: Here's what's coming—and some of it's already here. modern tools-first discovery: how people find businesses is fundamentally changing. Voice commerce: people making purchases through voice assistants. Hyper-personalization: customers expecting you to know them, remember them, anticipate them. Automation as standard: businesses without modern tools won't be able to compete on service speed and availability.

slides.title: The Vulnerable

risk_factors.risk: If that channel changes, you're invisible
Not discoverable by modern tools or voice
Can't scale, can't compete on speed

slides.narration: Which businesses are at risk? Those dependent on a single channel—if that channel changes, you're invisible. Website-only presence—not discoverable by modern tools or voice. Manual-everything operations—can't scale, can't compete on speed. No data capture—can't personalize, can't improve. Reactive-only mindset—always catching up, never leading. Sound familiar? Don't worry—all of this is fixable.

slides.title: The Resilient

resilience_factors.benefit: Found by voice and search tools

slides.narration: Which businesses will thrive? Those with multi-platform presence—visible wherever people look. discoverable—found by voice assistants and modern tools. Automated foundations—available twenty-four seven, responding instantly. Data-informed—constantly learning and improving. Community-connected—trust that survives any systems change. This isn't about being a tech company. It's about being visible, available, and efficient.

slides.title: The Foundation

foundation.what: A presence you own, not rented from platforms
Consistent flow of reviews and trust signals
modern tools handling routine, humans handling important
Capturing and learning from every interaction

slides.narration: Future-proofing starts with fundamentals. An authoritative home base—a presence you own, not rented from platforms that can change. Distributed visibility—present in multiple discovery channels. A reputation engine—consistent flow of reviews and trust signals. An automation layer—modern tools handling the routine so humans handle what matters. A data foundation—capturing and learning from every interaction.

slides.title: Your Action Plan

actions.action: Set up search tools for basic automation
Build review velocity and multi-platform presence

slides.narration: Here's your action plan. This week: claim your authoritative presence—get in the directory, set up your Alphasite. This month: set up an search tools for basic automation—responses, scheduling, FAQs. Next ninety days: build review velocity and expand your multi-platform presence. Ongoing: review your data, refine your approach, improve continuously. Small steps, big impact.

slides.title: Start Future-Proofing

content.headline: Ready to Build a Business That Lasts?

slides.narration: Ready to build a business that lasts? Get your personalized future-proof plan—specific steps for your business. Or take the readiness assessment to see where you stand today. The next five years will reward the businesses that prepare. Let's make sure that's you.
